_doc: Registry for tools/bench_node.sh — the repeatable bench-node runner. THIS file is the public-safe TEMPLATE (placeholders only). Copy it to tools/bench_nodes.json (GITIGNORED) and fill in REAL tailnet IPs / ssh users / repo paths there. Only 'sanitized_name' may ever appear in a committed artifact. Mirrors the fleet_endpoints.example.json convention.
_resolve_cmd_doc: EPHEMERAL hosts (#12): a node may carry an OPTIONAL 'resolve_cmd' instead of relying on a static tailnet_ip. When present, the runner runs it just-in-time (read-only; must print exactly one host/IP on stdout) to learn the CURRENT address of a host whose IP rotates per launch (e.g. a TTL-guarded GCP bench VM, #571), then takes the SAME ssh path. `info` never calls the resolver, so it stays offline-safe. Keep the REAL project/zone/instance inside resolve_cmd or its env in the GITIGNORED registry copy.
_safety: tools/bench_nodes.json is gitignored — real identity (tailnet_ip or resolve_cmd inputs, ssh_user, magicdns, repo_path, host_key) lives ONLY there.
Reachability is an SSH handshake, not ICMP/tailscale ping (a node can answer ping with no sshd).

host_key is PINNED here after bootstrap — the driver verifies against it instead of blind accept-new. An ephemeral host whose key rotates per provision leaves host_key empty and falls back to accept-new.

resolve_cmd must be a CHEAP read-only lookup (e.g. tools/gcp_bench.py --resolve-ip for a kept GCP VM, or a tailscale magicdns resolve) — it runs on every connecting subcommand; it must never provision or bill.
Every run writes lineage.json (utc datetime + fak version/commit + node + go) for traceability.

Results go to tools/_registry/bench-runs/ (gitignored). Promoting a result to a committed path needs a REAL redaction pass (CPU brand + go/git + hostname) — the shape-only scrub audit does NOT do that.

_onboarding._doc: Public-safe roster of the bench nodes this runner targets and where each stands (#922). The HARDWARE-CATALOG node roster is private; this block is the committed, sanitized view. 'pending' nodes have a placeholder entry below — onboarding = copy it into the gitignored tools/bench_nodes.json, fill in REAL identity, get the driver pubkey into the node's authorized_keys, pin host_key (ssh-keyscan -t ed25519), then `bench_node.sh <node> tests`. Every 'pending' node carries an 'issue' that tracks its onboarding — a dedicated child issue where one is filed (#10 laptop-from-desktop, #12 GCP GPU VMs), else the umbrella #922 itself (desktop, GPU server, which have no dedicated child yet) — so this roster is a navigable decomposition index for the umbrella.

roster.note: Apple M3 Pro arm64 — tests green, BenchmarkDecide ~317 ns idle.

roster.blocker: host-reachability — desktop answers ICMP but ships with no sshd; enable sshd + add driver pubkey, then pin host_key.

roster.note: RTX 4070 Laptop via Windows SSH -> WSL; `bench_node.sh laptop bench` passed from the desktop driver (bench rc=0, BenchmarkDecide ~1.1 us/op).

roster.blocker: private-registry-fill — dynamic-IP bridge is wired; copy the resolver entry into gitignored bench_nodes.json, set the real GCP project/zone/instance there or in env, then run bench_node.sh gcp-gpu ping after provisioning.
no-direct-ssh — The GPU server is reached via a private relay bridge, not a direct tailnet SSH handshake; needs a reachable sshd path before the driver can poll it.

nodes.name: gcp-gpu
nodes._ephemeral_doc: EPHEMERAL host (#12): tailnet_ip is empty because a TTL-guarded bench VM (#571) gets a fresh IP per launch. At runtime resolve_cmd asks the GCP provisioner for the CURRENT external IP. In the gitignored copy, set BENCH_NODE_GCP_INSTANCE/BENCH_NODE_GCP_ZONE and GCP_PROJECT/GCP_ACCOUNT as needed.
